questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: When the voltage applied to an X-ray tube is increased 1.5 times, the minimum wavelength of the continuous X-ray spectrum shifts by 26 pm . Find the initial voltage applied to the tube (approx)
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \lambda_1=\frac{h c}{e V_1} \\ & \lambda_2=\frac{h c}{e V_2} \\ & V_2=1.5 V_1 \\ & \left|\lambda_2-\lambda_1\right|=\frac{h c}{3 e V_1} \\ & 26 \times 10^{-12}=\frac{h c}{3 e V_1}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: There are two radio active substances $A$ and $B$ whose disintegration constants are $\lambda_A$ and $\lambda_B$ respectively. At $t=0$ the number of radioactive nuclei of $A$ is $N_{A 0}$ and that of $B$ is $N_{B 0}$. It is known that $\lambda_A\gt\lambda_B$ and $N_{A 0} \lt N_{B 0}$. The broken line shows the variation of $\ln \left(N_A\right)$ with respect to time for substance $A$. The graph of variation of $\operatorname{In}\left(N_B\right)$ with respect to time $t$ will be
questions.solution: $B$ will decay at slower rate than $A$. Also $N_B \gt N_A$ hence, $R$ graph will indicate variation of $\operatorname{In} N_B$ with $t$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
